title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.imageAlt: Authentic charcoal grilled tandoori chicken served with lemon wedges and cilantro, perfect for an Indian dinner.

description: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled is a quintessential Indian dish featuring succulent chicken marinated in yogurt and spices, then cooked to perfection over hot charcoal. The charcoal grilling imparts an incredible smoky flavor and crispy skin while keeping the meat incredibly tender and juicy. This vibrant and aromatic dish is a feast for the senses, perfect for a special dinner or a festive gathering.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make deep gashes on the chicken pieces (2-3 gashes per piece) to allow the marinade to penetrate deeply.

instructions.title: First Marinade Application
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with 1 tablespoon of lemon juice, 1 teaspoon of salt, and 1 tablespoon of Kashmiri red chili powder. Rub this mixture well into the chicken, ensuring it gets into the gashes. Let it rest for 15-20 minutes at room temperature.

instructions.title: Prepare Second Marinade
instructions.text: In another bowl, whisk together the thick yogurt, remaining 1 tablespoon of lemon juice, ginger-garlic paste, remaining 1 tablespoon of Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, remaining 1/2 teaspoon of salt, black pepper powder, mustard oil, and red food color (if using).

instructions.title: Combine and Marinate
instructions.text: Add the chicken from the first marinade to the second marinade. Mix everything thoroughly, ensuring each piece is well coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4-6 hours, or preferably overnight, for best flavor.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: About 30-45 minutes before grilling, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal to create a two-zone cooking area: one side with direct high heat and the other with indirect medium heat. Allow the coals to ash over and reach a steady temperature.

instructions.title: Grill the Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly over the medium-high heat zone of the grill. Cook for 5-7 minutes per side, turning frequently, until lightly charred and cooked through. Baste occasionally with melted butter or oil during the last 10-15 minutes of grilling to keep it moist and add flavor. The internal temperature should reach 165F (74C).

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5 minutes. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot with lemon wedges, mint chutney, or sliced onions.

tips: For the most authentic flavor and tender chicken, ensure the chicken marinates for at least 6 hours, or ideally overnight.
Maintain a consistent medium-high heat on your charcoal grill for even cooking and a beautiful char without burning. Use a two-zone setup.
Basting with melted butter or oil during the last stage of grilling not only adds moisture but also enhances the rich, smoky flavor and gives a glossy finish.
article:
Unleash the tantalizing aromas and vibrant flavors of India right in your backyard with a culinary masterpiece that’s both iconic and incredibly delicious: Tandoori Chicken. While often associated with the intense heat of a traditional clay tandoor oven, the magic of this dish can be beautifully recreated on a charcoal grill, imparting a smoky depth that elevates it to extraordinary heights. If you're searching for an unforgettable Indian dinner recipe, look no further than the rich, succulent experience of Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken. This guide will walk you through everything you need to know to master this beloved dish, turning your next dinner into a flavorful adventure.

## What is Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken?

Tandoori Chicken is a classic Indian dish made by marinating chicken in a mixture of yogurt and a vibrant array of spices, then traditionally cooked in a tandoor. The yogurt acts as a tenderizer, while the spices – including ginger, garlic, red chili, turmeric, cumin, coriander, and garam masala – infuse the chicken with an explosion of savory, tangy, and subtly spicy notes. What truly elevates this dish, however, is the method of cooking: Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken. The intense, direct heat of charcoal creates a beautiful char on the exterior, sealing in juices and imparting an unparalleled smoky flavor that simply cannot be replicated by an oven or gas grill. The result is chicken that is incredibly tender on the inside, with a wonderfully crisp, flavorful skin on the outside – a testament to the power of traditional grilling.

## Ingredients You Need

The process of making succulent Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken begins with selecting the right ingredients and preparing a potent marinade.

For the Chicken:
*   1.5-2 kg (3-4 lbs) whole chicken, cut into 8 pieces (or use bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs and drumsticks)
*   2 tablespoons lemon juice

For the Marinade:
*   1 cup (240g) plain full-fat yogurt (Greek yogurt also works, but thin it slightly)
*   2 tablespoons ginger-garlic paste (equal parts fresh ginger and garlic, crushed)
*   2 tablespoons red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color and mild heat, or regular for more spice)
*   1 tablespoon ground cumin
*   1 tablespoon ground coriander
*   1 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon black pepper
*   1-2 tablespoons Tandoori masala (optional, but enhances flavor and color)
*   1 teaspoon salt, or to taste
*   A few drops of red food coloring (optional, for authentic restaurant-style color)
*   2 tablespoons mustard oil or any neutral cooking oil (for basting)

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Once your chicken is ready and your grill is hot, it's time to create that irresistible Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken.

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Rinse the chicken pieces and pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make deep gashes (2-3 per piece) on the chicken to allow the marinade to penetrate deeply. Rub the chicken with lemon juice and set aside for 15-20 minutes.
2.  **Make the Marinade:** In a large bowl, combine the yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, black pepper, Tandoori masala (if using), salt, and red food coloring (if desired). Mix everything thoroughly until you have a smooth, vibrant paste.
3.  **Marinate the Chicken:** Add the chicken pieces to the marinade, ensuring each piece is fully coated. Use your hands to really rub the marinade into the gashes. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours, or preferably overnight (up to 24 hours) for the best flavor and tenderization.
4.  **Prepare Your Charcoal Grill:** Arrange your charcoal for a two-zone fire – one side with direct high heat and the other with indirect medium heat. Light the charcoal and let it burn until covered with a light gray ash, indicating it’s ready for grilling. Clean your grill grates and oil them lightly to prevent sticking.
5.  **Grill the Chicken:** Place the marinated chicken pieces over the direct heat first. Sear for 2-3 minutes per side to get a nice char and color. Then, move the chicken to the indirect heat side of the grill. Close the lid and cook, turning every 5-7 minutes, for about 25-35 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 74°C (165°F) and the juices run clear.
6.  **Baste and Finish:** During the last 10 minutes of cooking, baste the chicken generously with mustard oil or neutral cooking oil. This adds moisture and helps develop a beautiful crust. For an extra smoky kick, you can briefly move the chicken back over direct heat for a final char, being careful not to burn it.
7.  **Rest and Serve:** Once cooked, remove the Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5-10 minutes before serving. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring maximum tenderness.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Mastering the art of making the perfect Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken involves a few key techniques and insider tips that can elevate your dish from good to extraordinary.

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken sits in the yogurt-spice mixture, the more flavorful and tender it will become. Overnight marination is truly worth the wait.
*   **Full-Fat Yogurt:** Use full-fat yogurt as it contains more fat, which helps keep the chicken moist and prevents the marinade from curdling on the grill. If using Greek yogurt, thin it slightly with a tablespoon or two of water to achieve a good coating consistency.
*   **Score Deeply:** The gashes on the chicken are crucial for the marinade to penetrate deep into the meat, ensuring flavor in every bite.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Grill:** Grill in batches if necessary. Overcrowding lowers the grill temperature and can lead to steaming rather than grilling, preventing that coveted char.
*   **Temperature Control:** Keep an eye on your grill temperature. If the chicken is browning too quickly, move it further away from the heat source. If it's not charring enough, move it closer.
*   **Internal Temperature:** Always use a meat thermometer to ensure the chicken is cooked through to a safe internal temperature of 74°C (165°F).
*   **Rest the Chicken:** Resting the chicken after grilling is a non-negotiable step. It allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb juices, resulting in a juicier, more tender final product.

## Serving Suggestions

Whether you’re serving a simple weeknight meal or hosting a grand Indian feast, a plate of Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is always a showstopper. Here are some classic accompaniments:

*   **Bread:** Warm naan bread, fluffy roti, or flaky paratha are perfect for scooping up the flavorful chicken and any leftover juices.
*   **Chutney:** A refreshing mint-coriander chutney or a tangy tamarind chutney provides a wonderful contrast to the rich chicken.
*   **Salad:** A simple kachumber salad (diced cucumbers, tomatoes, onions, and cilantro with a lemon dressing) adds freshness and crunch.
*   **Onions & Lemon:** Serve with thinly sliced red onions and plenty of lemon wedges for a zesty squeeze.
*   **Rice:** Basmati rice, jeera rice (cumin rice), or a vegetable pulao makes a complete meal.
*   **Raita:** A cooling cucumber raita (yogurt with cucumber and spices) helps balance the spice.

## Variations and Substitutions

While Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is a classic for a reason, don't hesitate to experiment with variations to suit your taste or dietary needs.

*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the red chili powder to your preference. For less heat, use Kashmiri chili powder or paprika. For more heat, add a pinch of cayenne pepper.
*   **Marinade Flavors:** Introduce other spices like fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) for an earthy aroma, or a touch of black salt for a unique tang.
*   **Other Proteins:** The Tandoori marinade is incredibly versatile. It works wonderfully with boneless chicken pieces (adjust grilling time), fish fillets, paneer (Indian cottage cheese), or even lamb chops.
*   **Vegetarian Options:** Marinate thick slices of bell peppers, onions, zucchini, or mushrooms for a delightful vegetarian Tandoori option.
*   **Cooking Methods (if charcoal is not an option):** While charcoal grilling is highly recommended for its unique smoky flavor, you can also cook Tandoori chicken in a very hot oven (230°C/450°F) or on a gas grill. For oven baking, place chicken on a wire rack over a baking sheet and bake for 25-35 minutes, turning once, until cooked through. Broil for the last few minutes to get some char. For a gas grill, follow similar direct/indirect heat principles as charcoal.

Embrace the joy of cooking this spectacular Indian dinner recipe. The effort is minimal for the profound reward of succulent, smoky, and sensationally spiced Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken that will transport your taste buds straight to the streets of India. Enjoy the culinary journey!
